analysis_en: 小 attaches to 学生 inside 小学生 and identifies the junior school category.
第 comes directly before 一, turning the number into the ordinal 'first.'
们 follows 我 to form 我们, the plural first-person pronoun 'we.'
边 attaches to 那 and forms 那边, a location away from the speaker.
上 follows 桌子 and locates the book on the table's upper surface.
里 follows 房间 and marks the room's interior as the location.
会 precedes 开车 and presents driving as a learned skill.
能 precedes 来 and says present ability or circumstances allow him to come.
想 comes before 喝 and presents drinking tea as a wish.
要 directly precedes 买 and gives the intended purchase a firmer force.
可以 precedes 坐 and, with 吗, asks for permission to sit here.
可以 before 看 presents reading this book as permitted or acceptable.
看病 names the medical-care activity and follows 去医院 as its purpose.
上课 functions as the activity performed by 学生 at 学校.
什么 remains after 吃, exactly where the unknown food would appear in an answer.
哪儿 occupies the location position after 住 and asks for that missing place.
我们 is the plural first-person subject and includes the speaker.
她们 is the subject and refers to a group presented as all female.
这个 stands alone as the subject and points to one nearby item.
那些 is a plural distal demonstrative and identifies the books away from the speaker.
二 begins 二十, where it is part of the fixed cardinal number twenty.
两 appears before the classifier 件 to express a quantity of two.
本 stands between 一 and 书 because books use this dedicated classifier.
只 classifies the animal 猫 inside the phrase 一只猫.
杯 sits between 一 and 茶 and counts one cupful of tea.
杯 classifies two servings of water after 两.
日 follows the date number 一 in the formal calendar expression 五月一日.
岁 follows 十 and measures the son's age in years.
很 links the topic 今天天气 to the adjective 冷 and gives it an ordinary degree reading.
有一点儿 precedes 贵 and presents the degree of expense as mildly undesirable.
都 follows 我们 and distributes 是学生 over every person in that group.
都 follows the plural set 这些书 and makes 很好 apply to all of them.
在 immediately precedes 看书 and marks the reading as ongoing.
正在 before 吃饭 emphasizes that the meal is in progress at this moment.
再 precedes 来 and places another occurrence of coming on tomorrow's schedule.
再 before 说 requests another performance, while 一下 keeps the request light.
还 introduces 买了水果 as an additional purchase after 买了书.
也 before 是学生 marks the speaker as sharing the status already stated for 他.
不 before 喝 presents tea-drinking as a general negative choice or habit.
没有 precedes 上班 and denies that the past event occurred; no perfective 了 appears.
不要 stands before 说话 and turns the clause into a prohibition.
在 introduces 学校 as the location of 学习汉语 and the whole setting precedes the action.
在 introduces 三点 as the time setting for 上课.
和 introduces 朋友 as the companion who goes with the subject.
对 introduces 我 as the person toward whom the speaking is directed.
和 joins the two parallel objects 茶 and 牛奶 inside one object phrase.
和 coordinates 苹果 and 鸡蛋 as the two things bought.
的 links the possessor 我 to the possessed noun 书.
的 links 好看, meaning 'good to watch' here, to the noun 电影.
了 follows 买 and presents the two-book purchase as a realized, bounded event.
了 follows 吃 and views the dinner event as complete.
吧 at sentence end softens 回家 into an inclusive suggestion.
Sentence-final 了 presents rain as the new situation now in effect.
吗 converts the statement-order clause into a neutral yes-no question.
呢 closes a question already focused by 什么 and gives it a natural inquiring tone.
你呢 reuses the preceding topic and asks the same question about 你 without repeating the predicate.
Sentence-final 呢 highlights 看书 as the ongoing situation.
喂 opens the phone exchange by securing the listener's attention before the greeting.
喂 stands outside the clause and opens the call before the polite question.
爸爸妈妈 coordinates two kinship nouns as the plural subject of the sentence.
读书 and 写字 are parallel verb-object phrases joined by 和 as one object of 喜欢.
我的 modifies the head noun 书, and the whole phrase means 'my book.'
好看的, meaning 'good to watch' here, modifies the head noun 电影.
看 is the verb and 书 is its following object, together forming 看书.
吃 and its object-like component 饭 form the verb-object expression 吃饭 inside the predicate 在吃饭.
天气 is the internal subject and 很好 its predicate; 天气很好 is the proposition after 觉得.
他 is the internal subject of 是老师, and the whole proposition follows 知道.
三本 combines the numeral 三 with the book classifier 本 before 书.
两件 is a numeral-measure phrase that quantifies the following 衣服.
在学校 contains preposition 在 and object 学校; the whole phrase sets the location of 学习.
对我 combines 对 with its pronoun object 我 and identifies the target of speaking.
桌子上 combines the reference 桌子 with 上 to name its upper surface.
房间里 combines 房间 with 里 and serves as the place where the cat exists.
学生 is a noun in the subject position before the predicate 在看书.
他们 is the pronoun subject about which 都很高兴 is stated.
星期日 is the noun predicate giving the calendar identity of 明天 without 是.
十岁 is a quantity phrase used directly as the age predicate of 我儿子.
学习汉语 is the verbal predicate stating the subject's activity.
很漂亮 is an adjectival predicate describing 这件衣服 without using 是.
电影 is the noun object selected by 喜欢.
他们 is the pronoun object directly following 认识.
老师的 is a nominal possessive attributive placed before the head noun 书.
两本 is a numeral-measure attributive directly quantifying the head noun 书.
非常 is an adverbial immediately modifying the degree of 高兴.
今天 frames 学习 as the time adverbial.
在学校 frames 学习 as the place adverbial after the time expression.
一下 follows 看 and presents the requested look as brief and limited.
一下 follows 说 and makes the request to say your name brief and less abrupt.
我 is the subject and 学习汉语 is the verbal predicate centered on 学习.
有两本书 is a verbal predicate headed by 有 and states possession about 他.
很高兴 is the adjectival predicate describing 她, with no 是.
太热了 is the adjectival predicate describing today's weather condition.
五月一号 is a calendar-date nominal predicate directly following 今天.
十八岁 is an age expression functioning directly as the predicate of 我妹妹.
The weather event 下雨了 forms the whole message without a separate grammatical subject.
太冷了 presents the experienced condition directly, with no explicit subject-predicate division.
The sentence states the speaker's status and does not request an answer or action.
The sentence presents today's temperature as information in declarative form.
吗 follows the complete proposition 你是老师 and asks whether it is true.
吗 turns the statement-order event 他今天来 into a neutral yes-no question.
谁 fills the subject position and asks for the unknown person's identity.
什么 remains in the object position after 吃 and asks for the unknown food.
是不是 presents the affirmative 是 and negative 不是 as the two choices.
去不去 repeats 去 around 不 and asks the listener to choose between going and not going.
请 introduces the requested action 坐, with the listener understood as its subject.
不要 turns 说话 into a prohibition directed at the listener.
太漂亮了 presents the speaker's strong admiration rather than a neutral description.
真 intensifies 好吃 and gives the sentence an enthusiastic evaluative force.
是 links 他 to the class label 老师.
是 identifies the referent of 这 as 我的书.
有 links the possessor 我 to the possessed quantity 两本书.
没有 is the negative possession form and denies a computer in her possession.
学校前边 is the initial location, and 是 identifies 书店 as what occupies it.
医院后边 establishes the location before 是, and 饭店 names what is there.
桌子上 is the initial place, and 有 introduces the new entity 一本书.
房间里 sets the location, while 有 presents 两只猫 as existing there.
去商店 happens first, and 买水果 states the purpose of going.
去学校 is followed directly by 学习汉语, which gives the purpose of going.
坐出租车 precedes 去学校 and states the means by which the going happens.
开车 is the manner action and directly precedes the destination action 去医院.
给 takes 你 as the recipient object and 一本书 as the thing object, in that order.
我 is the first, recipient-like object of 给, followed by the transferred item 一个苹果.
The rain clause precedes the decision clause, and their causal relation is understood without a conjunction.
The illness and response form two related clauses; context supplies the causal link without an overt connector.
也 precedes the second 喜欢看书 and marks it as parallel to the first clause.
也 in the second clause makes 爸爸 share the teacher status already stated for 妈妈.
还 introduces 买了水果 as an additional purchase after the first clause.
还 adds 会写汉字 to the ability 会说汉语 already stated.
了 follows 看 and presents the movie-watching event as a bounded whole.
了 after 买 marks the computer purchase as realized and complete.
Sentence-final 了 presents cold weather as the new state now in effect.
Final 了 marks university-student status as a new situation, not a completed action.
在 stands directly before 看书 and marks reading as ongoing.
正在 directly precedes 吃饭 and focuses on the meal being in progress now.
在 marks 看书 as ongoing and final 呢 reinforces the current-situation frame.
正在 focuses the ongoing meal, and 呢 closes the clause as a current situation.
Final 呢 gives 看电视 the contextually ongoing reading without an overt preverbal marker.
呢 closes 做饭 and highlights cooking as the activity underway now.
二十元 is the standard-form money amount used directly as the book's price.
三块 uses everyday 块 as the yuan unit in the stated price.
第 prefixes 一 to form 第一, which precedes the book classifier 本.
第三 gives the student's ordered position and is followed by classifier 个.
五月 places the month number before 月 in larger-to-smaller date order.
一日 follows the month and marks the first day in formal date style.
星期日 is the conventional weekday expression for Sunday and serves as the predicate of 明天.
三点 gives the hour and 半 adds half an hour to form 3:30.
八点 states the hour and 十分 supplies the minute value before the action 上课.
面 attaches to 里 to form 里面, which locates the book in the schoolbag's interior.
面 follows 前 and forms 前面, the location in front of the shopping center.
左 forms 左边 after 商场 and identifies the shopping center's left side.
右 forms 右边 after 学校 and identifies the school's right side.
看看 repeats the one-syllable verb 看 and turns the instruction into a light suggestion.
看一看 places neutral-tone 一 between repeated 看 and presents the action as brief.
看了看 places 了 between repeated 看 and reports one brief look as realized.
休息休息 repeats the two-syllable verb and presents the proposed rest as brief and light.
可能 precedes 来 and presents his coming as possible, not certain.
可能 modifies 下雨 and marks the forecast as uncertain.
帮 and 忙 are separated so the internal object 忙 can take the quantity 一个.
游泳 appears together as the ordinary unsplit form of this verb-object word.
跳舞 appears together as the ordinary unsplit form of this verb-object word.
跑步 appears together as the ordinary unsplit form of this verb-object word.
为什么 comes before 学汉语 and asks for the reason behind studying Chinese.
为什么 precedes the negative predicate 没有来 and asks what caused his absence.
自己 refers back to 我 and emphasizes that the speaker personally does the cooking.
自己 refers back to 她, and 自己的 marks the book as her own.
那么 comes before 冷 and points to the notable degree of coldness.
那样 modifies 做 and refers to a more distant or previously mentioned manner.
这么 modifies 说 and points to the manner or degree evident in the current context.
这样 comes before 写 and points to the demonstrated way of writing.
每 selects students one by one, while 都 gives all of them the predicate 有一本书.
大大 repeats the one-syllable adjective 大 and gives the eye description a vivid quality.
大大小小 uses an AABB repetition of the contrasting adjectives 大 and 小 to cover varied sizes.
一万 uses 万 as the ten-thousand place-value unit before 名学生.
好多 directly quantifies 衣服 as a large, unspecified amount.
多 follows 二十 and leaves the exact number slightly above twenty unspecified.
条 classifies the long, flexible item 裤子 after 一.
位 respectfully classifies the person 老师 after 那.
间 classifies the room 教室 after 一.
名 gives a neutral formal count of the people 学生.
包 stands between 一 and 茶 and counts one packaged amount of tea.
包 follows 两 and counts two separate packets of medicine.
两次 follows 去过 and counts two separate experiences of going to China.
一次 follows 说 and requests one additional occurrence of the action.
多 precedes 漂亮 and, with final 啊, exclaims over the degree of beauty.
好 directly modifies 冷 and gives the statement an exclamatory degree.
最 precedes 快 and marks her speed as the highest in the relevant group.
一起 comes before 去 and groups 我们 as joint participants in the trip.
一起 modifies 学习 and presents the studying as a shared activity.
还 comes before 在睡觉 and says the sleeping continues at the present reference time.
已经 places the eating before the current reference point and emphasizes that it is already realized.
有时 marks taking the subway as an occasional rather than regular event.
有时候 is the full variant marking walking to school as occasional.
快 comes before 开始 and, with final 了, presents the start as close at hand.
快要 precedes 考试 and, with final 了, marks the exam as imminent.
正 directly precedes 上课 and locates the activity at the exact present moment.
经常 comes before 来 and marks coming here as a frequent action.
经常 precedes 上网 and presents it as a regular activity.
别 directly precedes 说话 and prohibits the action.
别 appears after the listener 你 and before 打开门, the action being prohibited.
再 stands before 去商场 and orders that trip after 吃了饭.
就 precedes 回家 and links it closely and promptly to 下课.
都 highlights that the late point 十点 has already been reached; final 了 marks the current situation.
就 narrows the purchase to the deliberately limited quantity 一本书.
还是 precedes 去上班 and maintains that action despite the obstacle introduced by 虽然下雨.
从 introduces 学校 as the place where the trip begins.
从 introduces 八点 as the starting time, while 到 introduces the endpoint 十点.
往 introduces 前 as the direction of 走.
往 introduces 学校 as the direction toward which he runs.
从 introduces 左边 as the directional source of the movement 过来.
从 introduces 这条路 as the route followed by the movement 走回家.
给 introduces 妈妈 as the beneficiary of the purchase.
给 introduces 朋友 as the person targeted by the phone call.
比 introduces 我 as the standard used to compare his height.
比 introduces 昨天 as the time used to compare today's temperature.
跟 introduces 朋友 as the companion who participates in the trip with 我.
跟 introduces 老师 as the counterpart from whom she learns Chinese.
因为 introduces 考试 as the reason within the clause whose main predicate is 没有去旅游.
因为 introduces 身体不好 as the reason for the main event 没有上班.
跟 joins the two parallel noun phrases 咖啡 and 绿茶 as objects of 喜欢.
跟 coordinates 苹果 and 鸡蛋 as two things bought.
但 introduces the lack of time as a contrast to the wish to go.
但是 introduces the high price as a contrast to the clothing's attractive appearance.
那 introduces 坐地铁 as the response appropriate to the situation in the first clause.
虽然 introduces the rain as a conceded fact that did not prevent the second-clause action.
还是 joins 茶 and 咖啡 as the two alternatives in the question.
因为 introduces 下雨 as the cause clause.
所以 introduces 我没有出去 as the result of that cause.
得 follows 跑 and introduces 很快 as the description of his running.
地 follows 高兴 and turns it into the manner description placed before 笑.
过 follows 去 and presents going to China as an experience in the speaker's life.
没有 negates the experience, while 过 remains after 坐 to define the experience being denied.
着 follows 开 and maintains the door's open state rather than presenting an opening event.
着 follows 拿 and presents the holding posture as continuing.
呢 closes the 怎么 question and adds the speaker's puzzled, emphatic reaction to the cold.
吧 turns the assertion into a request for confirmation of the speaker's tentative belief.
Sentence-final 的 asserts the speaker's confidence that 他会来 will hold.
Final 啊 gives the evaluation 真漂亮 an overt exclamatory reaction.
吃完 is a verb-complement phrase: 吃 is the verb and 完 supplies its result.
跑得很快 is a verb-complement phrase in which 得很快 describes the performance of 跑.
红色的 stands as the object of 喜欢; the relevant noun after 的 is understood from context.
老师买的 forms a nominal phrase referring to the understood thing bought by the teacher.
去商场 and 买衣服 share 我; the first movement supplies the purpose setting for the purchase.
坐地铁 and 去学校 share 他; the first phrase gives the means used for the second movement.
我们 is the object of 让 and simultaneously the understood subject of 看书.
我 is the object of 请 and the person who performs 买水果.
那位老师 is a complete nominal phrase functioning as the subject of the sentence.
我的两本书 combines possession, quantity, classifier, and noun into one subject phrase.
正在看电影 is a verbal phrase centered on 看 and functioning as the predicate.
去商场买衣服 is a verbal phrase combining movement and purpose after 想.
非常漂亮 is an adjectival phrase centered on 漂亮 and functioning as the predicate.
有一点儿冷 is an adjectival phrase whose degree expression modifies 冷.
什么的 follows three example objects and leaves similar items included but unstated.
什么的 closes the activity list and implies other similar interests without naming them.
还是 selects 坐地铁 as the preferred option, and 吧 makes the recommendation less forceful.
还是 presents 明天再去 as the considered preference, while final 吧 softens the group suggestion.
要 precedes 开始 and final 了 presents the movie's start as imminent.
快要 places the exam very near the present point, and final 了 marks the approaching change.
就要 emphasizes that 回来 is immediately forthcoming, while final 了 marks the imminent new situation.
都 and 了 frame 十点 as a late point already reached, motivating the request to go home.
都 highlights the full hour already reached; the final 了 presents that duration as the current situation.
衣服 is affected by 洗 rather than doing the washing, and 洗好了 states its resulting condition.
饭 is the thing affected by 做, while 做好了 reports its completed ready state.
星期五 is a nominal phrase serving directly as the predicate of 今天, with no 是.
二十岁 is the age phrase functioning as the predicate; Mandarin does not require 是 here.
学习汉语 is a verbal phrase placed before 的 to identify which students are meant.
妈妈做 is a subject-predicate phrase placed before 的 to modify 饭.
错 follows 写 and states that the writing produced an incorrect result.
懂 follows 听 and states that listening reached the result of understanding the teacher.
好 follows 做 and presents the meal as completed into a satisfactory, ready state.
会 follows 学 and marks knowing how to swim as the achieved learning result.
完 follows 吃 and marks the eating action as having reached its endpoint.
来 follows 拿 and directs the book toward the speaker's location.
去 follows 拿 and directs the item of clothing away from the speaker's viewpoint.
上 follows 走 and gives the walking an upward path onto the upper floor.
下 follows 坐 and presents the body moving down into a seated position.
进 follows 跑 and directs the running into the classroom.
出 follows 走 and gives the walking an outward path from the restaurant.
Full-tone 过 follows 走 and marks a path passing the shopping center.
回 follows 走 and gives the walking a returning path toward school.
出来 follows 走 and combines outward movement with 来, directing it toward the speaker's viewpoint.
出去 combines outward movement with 去, directing it away from the viewpoint.
过来 follows 走 and presents movement across toward the speaker's viewpoint.
过去 presents the walking as crossing away from the speaker's viewpoint.
回来 follows 走 and combines return with movement toward the current viewpoint.
回去 follows 走 and combines return with movement away from the current viewpoint.
进来 follows 走 and combines inward movement with 来, toward the viewpoint inside.
进去 combines inward movement with 去, away from the speaker's viewpoint.
起来 follows 坐 and presents upward movement into a seated position.
上来 combines upward movement with 来, toward the viewpoint.
上去 combines upward movement with 去, away from the viewpoint.
下来 combines downward movement with 来, toward the viewpoint.
下去 combines downward movement with 去, away from the viewpoint.
得 follows 跑 and introduces 很快 as the description of his running performance.
汉语 is placed before 说得很好, where 得 introduces the evaluation 很好.
八个小时 follows 睡了 and measures the duration of the sleeping event.
十分钟 follows 等了 and gives the length of the waiting.
两岁 follows 大 and measures the exact age difference in the 比 comparison.
十块钱 follows 贵 and measures the exact price difference in the explicit 比 comparison.
我 is the overall topic, while 身体很好 is the subject-predicate phrase serving as its predicate.
他 is the topic, and 眼睛很大 is the internal subject-predicate description.
还是 separates 茶 and 咖啡 as the two objects from which the listener should choose.
还是 links the two complete travel alternatives 坐地铁 and 坐公交车.
别 appears before 说话 and makes the clause a negative command.
The overt listener 你 precedes 别, which prohibits the following action 打开门.
是 links 这件衣服 to 红色的, a nominalized description of its color characteristic.
是 links the boy to 高个子, a nominal expression characterizing his build.
有 introduces 两年 as the duration reached since coming to China, and final 了 relates it to now.
有一年了 states that his Chinese study has reached a one-year duration as of now.
教室里 sets the place first; 坐着 presents 二十名学生 in a continuing seated posture.
床上 sets the location; 睡着 then introduces 一只猫 in a continuing sleeping state.
比 introduces 我 as the standard, and 高 states the property in which 他 exceeds that standard.
比 introduces 昨天 as the comparison standard, and 冷 gives today's greater degree.
大 gives the compared property, and 两岁 follows it as the exact measured difference.
贵 states the compared property, and 一点儿 follows it as the small degree of difference.
有 introduces 我 as the degree reached by his height.
这么 explicitly points to the height degree associated with 我.
没有 says the speaker's height does not reach the standard set by 他.
那么 points to that comparison standard's degree of height.
比我 appears before 跑, and 得快 evaluates the performance being compared.
跑得 comes first, and 比我 is placed inside the complement before 快.
是 stands before 昨天 and 的 closes the event, making the time the focused detail.
是 precedes 在学校 and 的 closes the event, focusing its location.
是 precedes 坐地铁 and 的 closes the event, highlighting the means of coming.
是 stands before 妈妈 and final 的 closes the event, focusing the person who bought the clothing.
我 is the object of 请 and the understood subject of 读这本书.
我 receives the instruction 叫 and also performs the following action 买水果.
我 is the object affected by 让 and the person who performs 打开门.
送给 forms the transfer predicate; 妈妈 is the recipient and 一件衣服 is the transferred object.
教给 marks information transfer; 我们 receives it and 一个新词 names what is taught.
是 frames the first travel clause, and 还是 introduces the second complete travel alternative.
还是 connects two clauses that differ in their time, with 回去 repeated in each option.
虽然 introduces the rain as a conceded obstacle, and 但是 introduces the action that happened despite it.
虽然 concedes the clothing's beauty, while 但是 introduces the decisive contrasting drawback.
因为 introduces 下雨 as the cause, and 所以 introduces 没有出去 as its result.
因为 frames the poor physical condition as cause, while 所以 introduces the resulting absence from work.
一 marks 到家 as the trigger, and 就 introduces 睡觉 as its immediate result.
一 introduces 看见我 as the trigger, and 就 places the smile immediately after it.
着 follows 开 and maintains the door's open state without focusing on the opening event.
拿着 presents the holding posture as a state that remains in effect.
着 follows 看 and extends the act of looking through the current interval.
着 follows 听 and presents listening to the teacher as an activity in progress.
过 follows 去 and presents going to China as part of the speaker's prior experience.
没有 negates the experience, while 过 after 坐 identifies the experience that is absent.
三楼 uses numeral plus floor unit as an ordinal label identifying floor three.
二号 uses numeral plus date label to identify the second day, not to count two items.
几天 gives a small, unspecified number of days before his return.
几分钟 gives the waiting a small but deliberately inexact duration.
多 comes between 十 and 分钟 and leaves the duration somewhat above ten minutes.
多 follows 二十 and precedes 个, giving an inexact count above twenty students.
多 follows 一个 and shows an extra fraction beyond one full hour but short of two.
多 follows 两年 and adds an unspecified fraction beyond two full years.
老 attaches directly to 朋友 and describes a friendship established over a long time.
老 before 同学 presents the person as a classmate from an earlier period.
家 attaches to 画 and forms 画家, the professional label for a painter.
子 is the neutral-tone noun suffix in the established word 桌子.
员 follows 服务 and forms 服务员, a label for a service worker.
东 combines with 边 after 医院 and identifies the school's direction from the hospital.
中间 follows the two reference places and locates the store between them.
需要 precedes 休息 and presents resting as a current need.
应该 comes before the recommended action 早一点儿睡觉 and frames it as good advice.
得, pronounced děi, directly precedes 上班 and marks it as necessary.
放假 is the sentence predicate and names the start or state of having time off.
见面 names the mutual meeting, while 跟朋友 identifies the other participant.
离开 takes 学校 as the place moved away from and forms the completed predicate.
完成 precedes 今天的工作 and presents that task as brought to completion.
怎样 stands before 写 and asks for the method of writing this character.
怎样 precedes 去学校 and asks which method of travel should fill that manner position.
谁 is non-interrogative, and 都 includes every possible person as someone permitted to come.
The two occurrences of 谁 match the unrestricted person in the condition with that same person's result.
什么 appears in the non-question expression 些什么 and refers to unspecified things rather than requested information.
哪儿 follows 在 inside a statement and identifies an uncertain place rather than asking where.
别人 is the recipient after 告诉 and means people other than those already involved.
咱们 is the subject and explicitly includes both speaker and listener in the suggestion.
别的 stands without a repeated noun and refers to a different book from the one mentioned.
其他 modifies 学生 and refers to the remaining students in the relevant group.
把 classifies the handled object 伞 after 一.
双 classifies 鞋 as a pair after 一.
封 stands between 两 and 信 as the dedicated classifier for letters.
层 follows 三 and counts the vertical floors of the building.
公斤 follows 两 as the weight unit measuring the apples.
碗 is borrowed from the container noun and measures one bowlful of rice.
碗 follows 两 and measures the rice by bowlful.
一口 follows 喝 and measures the drinking action as one mouthful.
两遍 follows 看 and counts two complete passes through the book.
